text: Did I seriously just hear a guy get mad because a game called TEAM Fortress 2 requires you to use teamwork to survive?  THAT'S THE WHOLE POINT, JACKASS.  It's designed so that a single hero carrying the team is unlikely, and you need to switch classes and tactics in order to better benefit the team at any given moment.  This all boils down to a guy who's upset that his lightning gun skills don't transfer to this game as well as he hoped, and he blames the game instead of his inability to adapt.

TF2 is not competitive at all and doesnt necessarly require skill

15. Sorry for spamming.
16. "This game makes me sick" the pixelation might. But I didn't hear you throw up.
17. "Matter of luck". Yes, you have a 2% chance within dealing 800+ damage to get "crits" which usually kill in one shot.

author: @masterlucee02

text:
6. Stop boasting about a game that has nothing to do with TF2. It's like comparing the PS1 to the PS3.
7. "No skill". Ever heard of aiming or logic? Playing Spy you have to get behind enemies to backstab them, really hard. Sap Engineer's buildings and get battered by him. Engineer himself needs to place his stuff somewhere good, and get spies before they sap his stuff. Heavy needs to stay out of open areas. Medic needs to not die to build up Ubercharge. Pyro needs to get close.
author: @masterlucee02

text:
Heavy = Good close and near a dispenser. Engie = Good anywhere. Medic = Best class imo. Sniper = Good long range and Spy = Good behind lines.
5. You don't even know much about the game, therefore a "n00b". I personally have never played nor touched UT. However I have played Serious Sam 2, which is a bit like it (fast-paced) and I can tell you those ARE skill games, but boring and dull. Team Fortress 2 has 9 classes=9 experiences. UT is just you running around shooting each other at high speeds.
author: @masterlucee02

text:
You sir, suck.
1. Get a better PC, I doubt you even have a good GPU (if you know what that is).
2. Stop swearing, it just extends the fact you suck at the game.
3. It's not all team, it's CLASS BASED. Like in CoD4 and 5, you get to customise your weapon layouts for close, long etc. Team Fortress 2 is that, but each class has it's own speciality.
4. ... Do I really have to go through them for you? Scout = Good in open areas. Solder = Good in high ground. Pyro = Good close. Demo = Good close.

text: This review is terrible. You can't just say the graphics are "crap" when they're quite obviously not. If you don't like them, fine, but it's not an objective review when you're saying that they're "crap". Also, you can easily get health or jump into the water to put out flames, and the game is incredibly well balanced. Each class has advantages and disadvantages and it's these that make the game fun. If all the classes were the same it wouldn't be any fun at all.
